name: Alpha Kappa Alpha sorority to host Women’s Wellness Summit next Friday
summary: Organizers said mental health will be a key focus of the summit.

name: A visit to the Las Vegas Sphere shows what's in store for Abu Dhabi
summary: From emojis winking on the skyline to concerts surrounding audiences in light, sound and motion, Sphere could redefine live entertainment in the UAE

name: ‘Long overdue’ anti-money laundering rules adopted by Nevada casino regulators
summary: Nevada gaming regulators are enhancing anti-money laundering practices inside casinos, as the state looks to play a more hands-on role in an area that has long been the domain of federal enforcement, following several recent high-profile violations by some of the biggest Las Vegas operators.

name: UNLV Rebel Girls & Company honored for back-to-back national titles during county meeting
summary: The co-ed team notches its seventh national title since 2013.

name: Catch future Las Vegas Strip musicians at this UNLV jazz show
summary: The Spring Jazz Festival approaches. The three-night celebration of jazz, featuring UNLV’s top student ensembles and guest artists, returns to Ham Hall from Monday through Wednesday.

name: Clark County commissioner moves money around multiple PACs to influence race to replace him
summary: Outgoing Clark County Commissioner Justin Jones has funneled thousands of dollars through various political action committees in support of his chosen successor.

name: The best way to sleep on a plane, according to science
summary: An eye mask, earplugs, and a same-day workout can all increase your odds.

name: UNLV preschool expanding to add four new classrooms and enroll 80 more students
summary: The Lynn Bennett Early Childhood Education Center on the UNLV campus is expanding. They're expected to be completed in the summer with a potential opening in the fall. More children also means more staff, giving more UNLV students an opportunity to work in a field they may want to pursue.

name: Vintage fashion trend offers sustainable alternative to fast fashion in Las Vegas
summary: Fast fashion may be keeping closets full, but it comes with a hidden cost. From water pollution to mountains of waste, globally fast fashion generates roughly 92 million tons of textile waste annually, according to the UN Environmental Program. Experts warn that the way people shop is taking a serious toll on the planet. In the valley, vintage shopping is emerging as a sustainable alternative.
